name: MITRE.LIN.T1561.001.RULE Disk Wipe: Disk Content Wipe
notes:
#WARNING - Can be noisy!

Following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rmdir -S unlink -S unlinkat -S rename -S renameat -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-file-delete

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when MITRE.LIN.T1107.RULE File Deletion match at least 50 times with the same Source IP in 1 minutes
thresholds: when MITRE.LIN.T1107.RULE File Deletion match at least 50 times with the same Source IP in 1 minutes
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1561.001.RULE Disk Wipe: Disk Content Wipe on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when MITRE.LIN.T1107.RULE File Deletion match at least 50 times with the same Source IP in 1 minutes
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1561.001.RULE Disk Wipe: Disk Content Wipe

Event description: Adversaries may partially or completely overwrite the contents of a storage device rendering the data irrecoverable through the storage interface.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1561/001
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
